full_prompt: Design a monolithic 4140 chromoly steel front-ski spindle (steering knuckle) for a collegiate clean-snowmobile IC-class benchmark, sized to deliver the benchmark minimum trail-speed (45 mph = 20.1 m/s), range (100 mi), and 500 ft acceleration (in <=12 s) capabilities on a 320 kg gross-weight prototype. The spindle is a single forged and machined AISI 4140 normalized + QT part (no welds, no bolted yokes) with integral upper and lower A-arm ball-joint sockets, integral steering-arm lug, and integral ski saddle stub. Geometry: overall bounding envelope 180 mm x 140 mm x 200 mm. Upper A-arm ball-joint socket: 25 mm hemispherical seat with 10 mm threaded retainer boss, M24 x 1.5 internal thread. Lower A-arm ball-joint socket: 28 mm hemispherical seat with 12 mm threaded retainer boss, M27 x 1.5. Steering-arm lug: 12 mm thick flat ear at 45 degrees with a 12 mm hole for the tie-rod rod-end, 80 mm from the spindle centerline. Ski saddle stub: 22 mm OD x 55 mm long cylindrical shaft downward from the lower socket, with a 10 mm through-pin hole perpendicular to its axis for the ski pivot. Kingpin axis (upper-to-lower socket): 150 mm vertical distance. Material AISI 4140 Q&T at 30 HRC: E 205 GPa, Poisson's ratio 0.29, density 7850 kg/m^3, yield 655 MPa, UTS 850 MPa; Charpy at -40 C (benchmark cold-start target) 27 J minimum per SAE J429 equivalent; endurance limit 0.5 * UTS * surface_factor 0.7 = 298 MPa at R=-1 for notched machined surface. Three load cases plus a cold impact case. Load Case 1 (cornering + bump): vertical 1800 N downward at the lower ball joint (2g on 220 kg rear-biased front corner weight ~90 kg front, plus 100% dynamic factor), combined with 1200 N lateral outward at lower ball-joint during 0.8g corner, upper ball joint constrained as pin, steering arm free. Load Case 2 (curb impact): 6.0 kN horizontal rearward load on the ski saddle stub pivot pin location, simulating a solid frozen stump strike at 45 mph per benchmark durability practice, both A-arm ball joints constrained. Load Case 3 (steering reaction): 800 N lateral at the steering-arm tie-rod hole (driver input + kingpin resistance at full lock with snow bogging), both ball joints constrained. Cold Impact Case: the curb impact of LC2 must also be evaluated at -30 C metal temperature (benchmark cold-start regime at -20 C ambient with wind chill) using the ductile-to-brittle transition knock-down: 4140 at -30 C has about 20% yield increase but toughness reduces; require minimum impact energy 20 J at -30 C. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1 operating, peak von Mises must not exceed 327 MPa (yield/2.0); (R2) under LC2 curb impact, peak stress 436 MPa (yield/1.5, short-duration impact); (R3) under LC3 steering, peak stress 327 MPa; (R4) fatigue: 10^6 cycles of LC1 scaled stress range 60%, local stress range at any ball-joint socket fillet must remain below 298 MPa; (R5) a linear buckling eigenvalue analysis under LC2 curb impact (compressive kingpin loading) must yield a first-mode factor of at least 2.5; (R6) spindle mass must not exceed 1.35 kg per typical benchmark mass budget; (R7) minimum Charpy impact toughness at -30 C must be >= 20 J (material specification gate, no FEA needed). Thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver STEP AP242 and Parasolid plus report.
short_prompt:
Design a monolithic 4140 Q&T chromoly-steel front-ski spindle (steering knuckle) for a collegiate clean-snowmobile IC-class benchmark (320 kg GVW, 45 mph trail-speed target). The spindle is a single forged-and-machined AISI 4140 part with integral upper and lower A-arm ball-joint sockets, an integral steering-arm lug, and an integral ski saddle stub; no welds, no bolted yokes. Apply yield/2.0 operating and yield/1.5 short-impact safety factors, plus a -30 C cold-impact toughness gate tied to SAE J429-equivalent Charpy practice.

Envelope: ~180 x 140 x 200 mm, kingpin axis 150 mm upper-to-lower socket.

Load cases.
- LC1 cornering + bump: 1.8 kN vertical + 1.2 kN lateral outward at the lower ball joint (2 g + 0.8 g corner), upper ball joint pinned, steering arm free.
- LC2 curb impact: 6.0 kN horizontal rearward at the ski saddle pivot (frozen-stump strike at 45 mph), both ball joints constrained; also a buckling check under compressive kingpin loading.
- LC3 steering reaction: 800 N lateral at the tie-rod hole (full-lock snow bogging), both ball joints constrained.
Cold impact: LC2 re-evaluated with a minimum Charpy toughness requirement of 20 J at -30 C.

prompt.title: Collegiate clean-snowmobile front-ski spindle benchmark (4140 chromoly)
prompt.brief: Monolithic forged 4140 knuckle with integral ball-joint sockets + steering arm + ski stub; -30 C cold-start impact gate.

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Cold toughness handled via material gate, not heat transfer.
exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Snowmobile NVH not governing at spindle level.
